source_note: Compiled from competitive SC2 meta documentation (Spawning Tool, Icy Veins, Liquipedia). 2025-2026 meta builds for TvT, ZvZ, ZvT matchups.

TvT.name: Reaper FE into Bio
TvT.description: Safe expand opener using a Reaper to scout, then transitioning into bio production with a natural expansion. The most common TvT standard opener.

steps.action: Tech Lab on Starport, Reactor on Barracks #2
Medivac from Starport, Marines from Barracks
Push or expand to 3rd base with bio army

TvT.name: Reaper FE into Cyclone-Marine
TvT.description: Reaper expand into early Cyclone harassment loaded into a Medivac. The Cyclone's Lock-On ability pins down SCVs and bio units. Transitions into standard bio after harass.

steps.action: Load Cyclone into Medivac, harass opponent main
Natural Orbital Command, continue bio production

TvT.name: 1-1-1 Banshee Cloak
TvT.description: One Barracks, one Factory, one Starport rush to get a cloaked Banshee harassing before the opponent has detection. Destroys SCV lines if undetected.

steps.action: Attack with cloaked Banshees, prioritize SCVs
Command Center at natural (if not taken yet)
Transition: add Barracks, Factory, begin bio or mech

TvT.name: Marine-Tank Timing Push
TvT.description: Classic TvT timing attack. Get a Siege Tank as early as possible with Marine support and push across the map. Forces opponent to respond or lose their natural base.

steps.action: Push: 1 Siege Tank + 4 Marines at 5:00 timing
Siege up outside opponent's natural, force response
Medivacs, continue pressure or macro up

TvT.name: CC First Greedy Macro
TvT.description: Build Command Center before Barracks for maximum economic advantage. High risk vs aggression but leads to overwhelming economy if opponent plays standard.

steps.action: Orbital on main, Orbital on natural

TvT.name: Proxy 2-Rax Marine Rush
TvT.description: Build two Barracks near the opponent's base hidden in fog of war and immediately flood Marines. Can end the game before opponent's bio is established if undetected.

steps.action: SCV scouts for proxy location near opponent
Marines begin production from both Barracks
Attack with 4-6 Marines before opponent has Bunker
If not winning: Refinery and Factory at home, tech up

TvT.name: Proxy Starport Hellion Drop
TvT.description: Reaper expand into a proxy Factory + proxy Starport. The Factory pumps early Hellions while the Starport's first unit is a Medivac (the bus). Hellions are loaded into the Medivac and dropped into the opponent's mineral line for SCV/Drone/Probe damage. Distinct from a 1-base Proxy 1-1-1: the player takes a 2nd Command Center for sustained production behind the harass.

steps.action: SCV moves out to scout proxy location near opponent
PROXY Factory (built far from main, near opponent)
Reactor on PROXY Factory (for double Hellion production)
Hellions x2 from PROXY Factory Reactor
MEDIVAC from PROXY Starport (FIRST Starport unit) — load 4 Hellions, fly to opponent's mineral line
Drop Hellions in opponent's main, Medivac heals to sustain harass
Marines from Barracks at home, Bunker at natural
Engineering Bay, Refinery, Siege Tank — transition into bio-tank macro
Add Barracks #2/#3 in main, begin 1/1 upgrades

TvT.name: Hellion Drop Harassment
TvT.description: Early Hellion production loaded into a Medivac to drop into opponent's mineral line. Hellions in light mode shred SCVs. Use Medivac heal to keep Hellions alive longer.

steps.action: Load 4 Hellions into Medivac, drop opponent's mineral line
Harass SCV line, use Medivac heal to sustain Hellions
Natural CC, add Barracks for bio transition

TvT.name: Bio with Liberator Support
TvT.description: Standard bio build using Liberators in Defender Mode to lock down territory and prevent bio runbys. Liberator zones cover choke points and ramps very effectively.

steps.action: Liberator #1, place in Defender Mode at natural ramp
3rd base, add Ghost Academy for late game

TvT.name: Full Mech (Thor-Cyclone-Hellion)
TvT.description: Full mech composition in TvT. Thors absorb bio damage with massive HP; Cyclones handle fast units; Hellions deal area damage. Strong vs bio but vulnerable to air transitions.

steps.action: Cyclone #1 from Tech Lab Factory, Hellions from #2
Begin Vehicle + Ship upgrades at Armory
Starport for Ravens (Point Defense Drone) and Vikings

TvT.name: Ghost-Marine-Medivac Composition
TvT.description: Transition to Ghost-based late game. Ghosts EMP opponent's Medivacs and bio ball; Snipe handles medium targets. Usually reached from a bio expand opener around 6:30+.

steps.action: 3-4 Ghosts in army, EMP bio armies before every engagement

TvT.name: 2-Rax Early Pressure
TvT.description: Two Barracks producing Marines early to apply pressure and deny opponent's scouting. Not a full all-in - creates early map control and delays their expansion timing.

steps.action: Supply Depot, move Marines across map
Attack natural or deny expansion with 4 Marines
Factory, Starport - transition to standard bio

TvT.name: SkyTerran BC-Raven Transition
TvT.description: Late game transition to Battlecruiser-Raven-Viking air superiority. Battlecruisers with Yamato Cannon delete Tanks and Thors. Ravens use Point Defense Drone vs bio. Counters both bio and mech.

steps.action: Fusion Core (begin when stable at 3 bases)
Yamato Cannon research at Fusion Core
Vikings for air control, Raven Interference Matrix vs Medivacs
Mass BCs, Yamato on Tanks, Thors, and high-value targets

ZvZ.name: Hatch First Standard Macro
ZvZ.description: The standard ZvZ macro opener: Hatchery at natural before Pool gives economic advantage if opponent doesn't all-in. Risky vs 12 Pool but dominant in macro mirror matchups.

steps.action: Roach Warren OR 3rd Hatchery based on scout
Spire for Mutalisks or Roach-Ravager transition

ZvZ.name: 12 Pool Speedling All-in
ZvZ.description: Very aggressive early pool to get Zerglings before opponent completes their Pool. Win condition is flooding the base before Spine Crawlers and Banelings are ready.

steps.action: Pull 3 drones off gas after 100 gas banked
Zergling Speed research immediately when pool done
If not winning: take natural expansion

ZvZ.name: Roach-Ravager 2-Base All-in
ZvZ.description: Build Roach Warren quickly and produce mass Roaches transitioning into Ravagers. Corrosive Bile destroys Spine Crawlers in 2 shots. Push before opponent gets Banelings or Mutas.

steps.action: Push: Roach-Ravager army attacks opponent's natural
Corrosive Bile on Spine Crawlers and Hatcheries
Win or transition to macro if opponent holds

ZvZ.name: Ling-Bane-Muta Standard
ZvZ.description: Classic ZvZ composition: Zerglings and Banelings on the ground with Mutalisks for air harass and map control. Strong vs Roach builds. The dominant mid-game ZvZ style.

ZvZ.name: 3-Hatch Before Pool Greedy Macro
ZvZ.description: Maximum economic expansion: 3 Hatcheries before Spawning Pool. Extremely risky vs aggression but gives unstoppable economy if opponent also plays greedy macro.

steps.action: Queen x3 (one per hatch), Spine Crawler x2 for defense
Zerglings x4 for defense and map presence
Roach Warren or Baneling Nest based on scout
Massive economy advantage - choose composition vs opponent

ZvZ.name: Baneling Flood Early Aggression
ZvZ.description: Fast Baneling Nest with mass Banelings attacking before opponent has anti-light units ready. Devastating vs Zerglings. Requires precise timing to hit before Roach Warren or Spine Crawlers.

steps.action: If held: take natural, macro up to Roach-Ravager

ZvZ.name: Roach-Hydra-Lurker Transition
ZvZ.description: Long macro game build: Roaches into Hydralisks then Lurkers. Burrowed Lurkers shred Ling-Bane armies trying to move across the map. Strong defensive and offensive tool.

steps.action: Hydralisks x4, Grooved Spines + Muscular Augments upgrades
Burrow Lurkers at ramps and key positions

ZvZ.name: Queen Walk Aggression
ZvZ.description: Produce extra Queens and walk them across the map for early aggression. Queens deal high damage vs Zerglings and can overwhelm defenses if opponent doesn't have Banelings ready.

steps.action: Queen x1 at home, then 3 more Queens back to back
Attack opponent's natural with Queens + Zerglings
More Zerglings produced at home hatch
If denied: Roach Warren or Lair at home, transition to macro

ZvZ.name: Mutalisk Timing Switch
ZvZ.description: Fast Spire off 2 bases to get Mutalisk harassment at 6:00. First 6 Mutalisks attack before opponent can react. Forces Spore Crawlers everywhere and denies map control.

steps.action: Lair (ASAP - this is the priority spend)
Attack with first Mutas while droning at home
Mutalisk x12-15, dominate the skies, take 3rd base

ZvZ.name: Nydus Roach All-in
ZvZ.description: Build a hidden Nydus Worm inside the opponent's base and pour Roaches through it. Completely bypasses all surface defenses. Devastating if undetected.

steps.action: Send Overlord to scout and find hidden placement spot
Place Nydus Worm in opponent's base near mineral line
Pour 8-10 Roaches through Nydus into opponent's base
Target Hatcheries, Spawning Pool, Queens first
Continuously send units through - overwhelm base

ZvZ.name: Ravager-Roach Timing Attack
ZvZ.description: Ravagers with Corrosive Bile destroy Spine Crawlers and Hatcheries. Push at 5:00-6:00 window before opponent has Lurkers or Mutalisks. Bile hits Spines for ~50% HP each.

steps.action: More Roaches x6 total, Glial Reconstitution (speed)
Corrosive Bile on Spine Crawlers and Queens
Keep production flowing from home - reinforce push

ZvZ.name: Hydra-Lurker-Viper Late Game
ZvZ.description: End-game Zerg composition. Lurkers hold territory; Hydralisks deal ranged damage; Vipers Abduct Lurkers and use Blinding Cloud to counter stacked Mutalisks. Dominant in long games.

steps.action: Full Hydra-Lurker-Viper army dominates late game

ZvT.name: Standard Macro Hatch-Gas-Pool
ZvT.description: Safe and standard ZvT opener: natural Hatchery first then Pool. Gives economic advantage against bio while being safe enough vs standard Reaper FE opens from Terran.

steps.action: Baneling Nest + Roach-Ravager production based on scout

ZvT.name: Pool First Ling-Bane Aggression
ZvT.description: 12-14 Pool for fast Zerglings to deny the Reaper then Banelings to crush Marine-Marauder armies. Banelings deal massive damage to bio. Very strong vs standard Terran bio expand.

steps.action: Zergling Speed immediately when pool done
Zerglings x4 (deny Reaper scout, chase off map)
Attack Terran's natural: Banelings on Marines, Lings cleanup
If held: take 3rd base, continue Ling-Bane production

ZvT.name: Roach-Ravager Timing Attack
ZvT.description: Mass Roaches transitioning into Ravagers for a 5:30-6:00 timing. Corrosive Bile destroys Bunkers and sieged Tanks. Punishes greedy Terran expands and bio builds with no Siege Tanks.

steps.action: More Roaches - total force: 8 Roach + 4 Ravager
Corrosive Bile on Bunkers and Tanks, Roaches tank for Ravagers
If winning: destroy natural. If held: macro to 3rd hatch

ZvT.name: Ling-Bane-Muta Standard
ZvT.description: The classic ZvT mid-game composition. Ling-Bane handles ground bio armies; Mutalisks harass and force expensive Turrets; together they create a two-front problem Terran struggles to solve.

steps.action: Mutalisk x12, harass mineral lines and force Turret spending
Carapace + Melee/Missile upgrades, Hive transition if long game

ZvT.name: Hydra-Lurker vs Bio
ZvT.description: Strong counter to Marine-Medivac bio play. Burrowed Lurkers shred Marine clusters; Hydralisks deal ranged damage to Medivacs and bio reinforcements.

steps.action: Burrow Lurkers at key choke points, deny all pushes
Hive, Vipers for Blinding Cloud vs bio deathball

ZvT.name: Ultralisk-Baneling Deathball
ZvT.description: Late game Zerg standard: Ultralisks tank massive bio damage; Banelings detonate on Marines for area kills; Zerglings clean up. The dominant Hive-tier ZvT composition.

steps.action: Hive (transition from Lair when at 3+ bases stable)
Ultralisk x4-6 + Banelings + Zerglings deathball assembled
Engage: Ultralisks lead, Banelings flank and detonate on bio

ZvT.name: Fast Mutalisk Harassment
ZvT.description: Fast Spire off 2 bases to harass at 5:30-6:00. Forces Terran to build Turrets (expensive) and delay push timing, giving Zerg time to take 3rd base and macro up.

steps.action: Lair (fast priority - spend gas here first)
Attack SCV mineral lines, hit Turret-less bases first
Mutalisk x12-15, force Turret spending everywhere
Transition to Corruptor or Broodlord vs mech

ZvT.name: Swarm Host vs Turtle Mech
ZvT.description: Counter to Terran Mech (Tank-Thor-Hellion). Swarm Hosts produce free Locusts, forcing Mech to constantly spend energy and fight. Combine with Vipers to Abduct Thors and Tanks.

steps.action: Swarm Hosts x4 from Infestation Pit
Position Swarm Hosts outside Mech line, spawn Locusts constantly
Vipers x3: Abduct key Mech units, Blinding Cloud on Thor clusters
Continue SH + Viper pick-offs, take 4th base for economy

ZvT.name: Ling-Infestor vs Mech
ZvT.description: Infestors support Zerglings vs Terran mech. Fungal Growth immobilizes Hellion clusters and bio; Infested Terrans deal splash damage. Strong mid-game vs Tank-heavy compositions.

steps.action: Fungal Growth on Hellions and bio clusters to immobilize
Neural Parasite on Thors and BCs, transition to Hive

ZvT.name: Nydus Network All-in
ZvT.description: Hidden Nydus Worm inside Terran's base bypasses wall and Bunker defense entirely. Pour Roaches directly into the mineral line. Very strong on maps with back-door placement spots.

steps.action: Send Overlord to scout Terran main for placement
Place Nydus Worm hidden in Terran main near mineral line
Target Command Centers and Barracks first
Continuously send units - if held, transition to macro

ZvT.name: Fast Ravager 5:00 Timing
ZvT.description: Early Ravagers with Corrosive Bile hit Terran's natural before defensive setup is complete. Bile destroys a Bunker in 2 shots. Timing hits at 4:30-5:00 before Siege Tanks arrive.

steps.action: Zerglings x4 for flanking, Glial Reconstitution
Move out with 4 Roach + 3 Ravager + Zerglings
Corrosive Bile on Bunker (2 shots = destroyed)
Bile on CC, Lings flank the Marines
Win condition: destroy natural. If held: drone up to 3 bases

ZvT.name: 4-Hatch Economy vs Bio Expand
ZvT.description: Greediest economic opening vs Terran Reaper FE. 4 Hatcheries gives massive Drone advantage for long macro games. Only viable when scout confirms standard bio expand, not an all-in.

steps.action: Spine Crawler x1 at natural (defense vs Reaper)
Massive Ling-Bane-Roach army with dominant economy
Superior resource income leads to overwhelming late game
